scenario: Iris Insurance starts 11000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8 from a curl integration. The team prefers the HTTP REST API to start a process instance by bpmnProcessId with a JSON variables payload over installing a language SDK in a constrained environment.
question: Which Camunda 8 REST API endpoint creates a new process instance?

options.text: POST /v2/process-instances with a JSON body containing processDefinitionKey or bpmnProcessId and a variables object as the payload root
POST /api/v1/instances with a JSON body containing the bpmnProcessId only; variables are supplied via subsequent PUT /api/v1/instances/{id}/variables
PUT /process/{bpmnProcessId}/start with no body; the broker uses the most recent set of process variables from the previous instance of the definition
POST /zeebe/grpc with a base64-encoded gRPC payload; the REST API tunnels gRPC requests as a fallback for HTTP-only environments without protocol negotiation

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8's REST API uses POST /v2/process-instances with processDefinitionKey or bpmnProcessId and a variables object to start an instance.

b.text: Incorrect. /api/v1/instances is not the documented Camunda 8 path; /v2/process-instances is the canonical surface.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no implicit reuse of prior variables and no PUT-style start endpoint; the documented endpoint is POST /v2/process-instances.

d.text: Incorrect. The Camunda 8 REST API is native HTTP/JSON, not a gRPC tunnel; gRPC is a separate Zeebe protocol.

explanation: Camunda 8 REST API: POST /v2/process-instances starts an instance with processDefinitionKey or bpmnProcessId plus a variables object.
